items.title: Boston Dynamics' Robot Dog Can Now Read Gauges, Spot Spills, and Reason
items.description: Boston Dynamics has integrated Google DeepMind into its robotic dog Spot, giving it more autonomous reasoning for industrial inspections like spotting spills and reading gauges. Spot can also now recognize when to call on other AI tools. IEEE Spectru

items.title: US Jobs Too Important To Risk Chinese Car Imports, Says Ford CEO

items.title: Snapchat Blames AI As It Cuts 1,000 Jobs

items.title: Norway Man Cured of HIV With Brother's Stem Cells

items.title: Sony Boss Urges Theaters To Stop 30 Minutes of Trailers and Ads Before Movies

items.title: Ford EV and tech chief leaving automaker
items.description: Doug Field had stints at Apple and Tesla before joining Ford in 2021.

items.title: Wait, could they still actually break up Live Nation?
items.description: A federal jury found that Live Nation has acted illegally as a monopoly -- but the company just came to a tentative settlement with the DOJ last month.

items.title: Monarch Tractor’s collapse ends with an acquisition by Caterpillar
items.description: The startup had faced mounting pressure from dealers, farmers, and a co-founder who complained that Monarch's tech didn't work properly.

items.title: OpenAI updates its Agents SDK to help enterprises build safer, more capable agents
items.description: OpenAI has expanded the capabilities of its agent-building toolkit, as agentic AI continues to grow in popularity.

items.title: Hightouch reaches $100M ARR fueled by marketing tools powered by AI
items.description: The startup says it grew its ARR by $70 million in just 20 months after launching an AI agent platform for marketers.

items.title: LinkedIn data shows AI isn’t to blame for hiring decline… yet
items.description: LinkedIn says hiring is down 20% since 2022, but blames higher interest rates — not AI — for the slowdown.

items.title: Feds will require data centers to show their power bills
items.description: In a first, the U.S. Energy Information Agency said it will require data centers to disclose details of their energy use.

items.title: AI learning app Gizmo levels up with 13M users and a $22M investment
items.description: Gizmo, an AI-powered learning platform, has attracted more than 13 million users and just secured $22 million in Series A funding.

items.title: Can AI judge journalism? A Thiel-backed startup says yes, even if it risks chilling whistleblowers
items.description: Objection, a Thiel-backed startup, aims to use AI to judge journalism, letting users pay to challenge stories. Critics warn it could chill whistleblowers and reshape how media accountability works.

items.title: Google rolls out a native Gemini app for Mac
items.description: You can share anything on their screen with Gemini to get help with what they're looking at in the moment, including local files.

items.title: This Khosla-backed autonomous pod startup just raised $170M — now it’s aiming for more
items.description: Glydways is in talks to raise another $250 million as it prepares to launch three pilot programs.

items.title: Accel raises $5B to back late-stage bets
items.description: Accel announced on Tuesday that it raised $5 billion in fresh capital to back late-stage companies building AI.

items.title: India’s vibe-coding startup Emergent enters OpenClaw-like AI agent space
items.description: Emergent's Wingman lets users manage and automate tasks through chat on platforms like WhatsApp and Telegram.

items.title: Anthropic shrugs off VC funding offers valuing it at $800B+, for now
items.description: VCs are frothing at the mouth to write more checks to Anthropic at a valuation that matches or beats OpenAI's.

items.title: Motorola sues social platforms and creators over posts, raising speech concerns in India
items.description: Motorola has sought a permanent injunction restraining the defendants from publishing or sharing what it describes as false or defamatory content about its products, including reviews, videos, comments, and boycott campaigns.

items.title: Airwallex is about to take on Stripe and the rest of the payments industry — in the physical world
items.description: Airwallex, valued at $8 billion, is launching a point-of-sale product that lets businesses accept in-person payments in multiple countries via a single platform.

items.title: After sale of its shoe business, Allbirds pivots to AI
items.description: Allbirds is ditching wool sneakers for AI servers, rebranding as NewBird AI after locking in a $50M convertible financing facility.

items.title: Sweden blames Russian hackers for attempting ‘destructive’ cyberattack on thermal plant
items.description: Sweden's minister for civil defense said Russian hackers are "now attempting destructive cyber attacks against organizations in Europe."
